codes.title: Roll Anime to Fight Codes

codes.whatIs: What Are Roll Anime to Fight Codes?
codes.whatIsDescription: Codes are promotional strings the developer releases through the official Roblox experience and the Another Slop community. Entering a code in the in-game Codes menu grants free rewards such as extra rolls, gems, and progression boosts.

whySteps.step1Description: We monitor the official Roblox page and community channel so every listed code reflects its real active or expired status.
whySteps.step2Description: Follow the exact menu path and entry format to avoid typos, extra spaces, or capitalization errors that block redemption.
whySteps.step3Description: See what each code grants before redeeming, so you know which free rolls, gems, or boosts are heading to your account.

guide.metaTitle: Roll Anime to Fight Guide - Beginner Tips, Controls & Waves
guide.metaDescription: Master Roll Anime to Fight with beginner guides, controls, wave and checkpoint strategies, and boss fight tips for faster progression on Roblox.
guide.whatIs: What Is a Roll Anime to Fight Guide?
guide.whatIsDescription: Guides walk you through the core loop — rolling fighters, equipping a team, surviving waves, merging duplicates, and clearing checkpoints — with practical steps you can follow during your first session.

whySteps.step1Description: Get from your first roll to a working team without wasting currency on the wrong fighters.
whySteps.step2Description: Learn movement, placement, and combat controls so you react faster when waves get crowded.
whySteps.step3Description: Approach checkpoints and boss fights with the right team composition instead of guessing your way through.

tiers.whatIs: What Is the Roll Anime to Fight Tier List?
tiers.whatIsDescription: The tier list compares fighters across S, A, B, and C ranks based on sustained damage, attack speed, enemy coverage, and how well they hold up as enemy health scales at higher checkpoints.

whySteps.step1Description: Fighters are judged by real wave damage and efficiency, not by rarity color alone.
whySteps.step2Description: Separate rankings for early, mid, and late game show which fighters peak at each progression stage.
whySteps.step3Description: Use the ranks to decide which duplicates to merge and which fighters deserve your resources first.

fighters.whatIs: What Are Roll Anime to Fight Fighters?
fighters.whatIsDescription: Fighters are the anime units you roll, equip, and send into battle. Each one has its own rarity, damage, range, cooldown, and acquisition method, and the roster spans standard, secret, and limited units.

whySteps.step1Description: Check damage, DPS, range, and cooldown for any fighter before committing resources to it.
whySteps.step2Description: Understand how rarity tiers affect value and which units are worth keeping or merging.
whySteps.step3Description: Track hard-to-find secret and limited fighters so you know what to chase and when they appear.

rolling.title: Roll Anime to Fight Rolling Guide
rolling.description: Roll Anime to Fight rolling guide covering roll rates, luck, rare fighter odds, rolling strategy, and how to get more rolls efficiently.
rolling.metaTitle: Roll Anime to Fight Rolling Guide - Rates, Luck & Odds
rolling.metaDescription: Learn Roll Anime to Fight roll rates, luck bonuses, rare fighter odds, and rolling strategy to pull stronger units and get more rolls faster.
rolling.whatIs: What Is Rolling in Roll Anime to Fight?
rolling.whatIsDescription: Rolling is how you obtain new fighters. Each roll follows set rates and is shaped by luck bonuses, so understanding probabilities and strategy helps you pull rare and mutated units more reliably.

whySteps.step1Description: See the real probability behind each rarity so you can plan your rolls instead of relying on luck.
whySteps.step2Description: Apply luck boosts and the right timing to raise your odds of pulling rare and mutated fighters.
whySteps.step3Description: Follow proven rolling strategy and methods to earn more rolls and avoid wasting currency.

merging.title: Roll Anime to Fight Merging Guide
merging.description: Roll Anime to Fight merging guide covering duplicate fusion, leveling, max fighter level, merge priorities, and resource efficiency.
merging.metaTitle: Roll Anime to Fight Merge Guide - Leveling & Priorities
merging.metaDescription: Master Roll Anime to Fight merging: fuse duplicates, raise levels, reach max fighter level, set merge priorities, and spend resources efficiently.
merging.whatIs: What Is Merging in Roll Anime to Fight?
merging.whatIsDescription: Merging combines duplicate fighters to raise a unit's level and combat strength. It is the main way to turn repeated rolls into permanent team power, so choosing the right copy and merge order matters.

whySteps.step1Description: Feed matching duplicates into your best copy to push fighters toward max level without wasted merges.
whySteps.step2Description: Decide which fighters deserve merges first and which low-rarity units are not worth the investment.
whySteps.step3Description: Keep valuable mutations on separate branches and only merge duplicates that truly improve your active team.

teams.metaTitle: Roll Anime to Fight Best Teams - Builds for Every Stage
teams.metaDescription: Build the best Roll Anime to Fight teams for early game, wave survival, and mutations, with team building tips and how to equip more fighters.
teams.whatIs: What Makes a Strong Roll Anime to Fight Team?
teams.whatIsDescription: A strong team balances focused damage, fast attackers, wide enemy coverage, and useful mutations. The right composition changes as you progress from early checkpoints to high-wave runs.

whySteps.step1Description: Follow ready-made early, mid, and high-wave teams tuned for where you are in progression.
whySteps.step2Description: Learn which roles to fill and how to mix focused and wide damage for consistent wave clears.
whySteps.step3Description: Find out how to expand your active lineup so more of your strongest units can fight at once.

updates.whatIs: What Are Roll Anime to Fight Updates?
updates.whatIsDescription: Updates track version changes, new fighters, balance adjustments, events, and system reworks. Staying current helps you adapt your team and rolls to whatever the latest patch changes.
updates.why: Why Follow Roll Anime to Fight Updates?

whySteps.step1Description: See what the newest update changed the moment it goes live, from new units to balance tweaks.
whySteps.step2Description: Review past updates in order so you understand how the game and its fighters have evolved.
whySteps.step3Description: Find the right Reddit and Discord communities to catch announcements and discuss updates with other players.

codes.title: Roll Anime to Fight Codes
codes.intro: Roll Anime to Fight includes an in-game Codes menu for promotional rewards. Enter codes exactly as published, because spelling, capitalization, and extra spaces can prevent redemption.

statusCards.message: We are actively monitoring the official Roblox experience and the Another Slop community page. No active code has been published yet, so check back as the developer releases new Roll Anime to Fight codes.
No expired code has been archived yet. Once codes are released and later retired, each one will be listed here alongside the reward it used to give.

redeemSteps: Launch Roll Anime to Fight on Roblox.
Open the Codes option from the in-game menu.
Enter the code exactly as it was published.
Press the redeem button and collect the reward.

beginner-guide.title: Roll Anime to Fight Beginner Guide
beginner-guide.intro: The core loop is simple: roll anime fighters, equip your strongest team, survive enemy waves, merge duplicate fighters, and keep pushing checkpoints. Early progress is faster when you improve a small working team instead of spreading resources across every fighter.

steps.title: Roll Your First Fighters
steps.description: Use the starting roll system to obtain your first anime fighters. Keep enough usable fighters to fill your active team before merging duplicates.

steps.title: Compare Combat Performance
steps.description: Check each fighter's damage, attack speed, attack coverage, level, rarity, and mutation. A lower-rarity fighter with better practical damage can stay useful during early waves.

steps.title: Build a Balanced Team
steps.description: Combine fighters that deal strong focused damage with fighters that cover larger groups of enemies. Avoid filling every slot with slow attackers that do the same job.
steps.priority: Mix focused damage and wider attacks

steps.title: Start the Endless Waves
steps.description: Enter battle and let your equipped fighters clear incoming enemy waves. Watch which fighters consistently contribute damage and which ones fall behind.
steps.priority: Use battle results to improve the team

steps.title: Merge Duplicate Fighters
steps.description: Merge duplicate anime fighters to raise their levels and improve their combat value. Do not merge a duplicate that is still needed to complete your active lineup.

steps.title: Reach New Checkpoints
steps.description: Push through increasingly difficult waves to reach higher checkpoints and earn larger progression rewards. Return to rolling and merging whenever your team can no longer clear waves efficiently.
steps.priority: Upgrade before repeatedly retrying a wall

steps.title: Protect Rare Fighters and Mutations
steps.description: Review rare fighters and mutation effects before using them as merge material. A useful mutation can make a fighter more valuable than another unit with higher base rarity.

anime-tier-list.title: Roll Anime to Fight Anime Tier List
anime-tier-list.intro: A fighter's rarity is only one part of its value. The strongest units combine consistent damage with useful attack speed or coverage and keep performing after enemy health scales up at higher checkpoints.

tiers.label: High-Wave Core
tiers.profile: Fighters with top sustained damage, efficient attack speed, strong enemy coverage, or a powerful mutation that stays useful during difficult waves.
tiers.bestUse: Permanent core team and major upgrade investment
tiers.mergeRule: Keep every strong copy until the main team is complete

tiers.label: Strong Main-Team Fighters
tiers.profile: Fighters that excel in focused damage, attack speed, or wider attacks but do not combine every top-tier advantage.
tiers.bestUse: Early, mid-game, and most checkpoint teams
tiers.mergeRule: Level the best copy and merge unused duplicates

tiers.label: Consistent Progression Fighters
tiers.profile: Consistent fighters that clear early and middle waves but begin losing efficiency when enemy health and wave pressure increase.
tiers.bestUse: Starter teams, temporary upgrades, and filling missing roles
tiers.mergeRule: Replace gradually when stronger fighters are rolled

tiers.label: Early-Game Fillers
tiers.profile: Low-output fighters with limited damage, slow attacks, weak coverage, or no mutation advantage.
tiers.bestUse: Filling empty team slots during the first rolls
tiers.mergeRule: Use spare copies as merge material after obtaining replacements

best-teams.title: Roll Anime to Fight Best Teams
best-teams.intro: The best team is not always the lineup with the highest visible rarity. Strong teams combine upgraded fighters, fast or consistent damage, wider enemy coverage, and useful mutations so that different wave conditions are handled efficiently.

teams.name: Early-Game Starter Team
teams.strategy: Fill every available team position before heavily merging units. Replace weak starter fighters one at a time as better rolls appear.

teams.name: Mid-Game Balanced Team
teams.strategy: Balance focused damage for harder enemies with wider attacks for crowded waves. Invest merges into fighters that stay active and useful throughout each battle.
teams.priority: Main damage dealer, coverage fighter, mutation holder

teams.name: High-Wave Damage Team
roles: Fully merged or strongly mutated units
teams.strategy: Remove fighters whose damage falls sharply behind the rest of the lineup. Prioritize proven high-wave performance instead of keeping a unit solely for higher rarity.

teams.name: Mutation-Focused Team
teams.strategy: Compare mutated fighters directly with higher-rarity alternatives during the same wave range. Keep mutations that noticeably improve damage, speed, or overall clearing ability.
teams.priority: Best mutation and highest-performing fighter combination

checklist.title: Team-Building Checklist
checklist.stage: Before starting a new wave run
checks: Every available team position is filled
The lineup includes both focused and wider damage
Useful mutations are equipped instead of merged away
Duplicate copies have been merged into active fighters
The strongest units receive upgrades first

rolling-and-rarities-guide.title: Roll Anime to Fight Rolling and Rarities Guide
rolling-and-rarities-guide.intro: Every roll can produce a new fighter with its own rarity, damage, range, cooldown, trait, and mutation. Compare the complete fighter card instead of choosing units by rarity color alone. Mythic and Legendary fighters are valuable pulls, while mutated versions such as Gold, Diamond, Nen, and Titan should be preserved as separate upgrade lines.

rows.stats: Level, DPS, RNG, CD, trait and mutation
rows.use: Equip strong Mythic fighters immediately and preserve every useful duplicate for merging.

rows.stats: Level, DPS, RNG, CD, trait and mutation
rows.use: Use Legendary fighters while building a Mythic roster. Merge matching duplicates instead of discarding them.

rows.stats: A mutation label appears with the fighter, including Gold, Diamond, Nen, or Titan
rows.use: Keep mutated copies separate from normal copies and develop the strongest mutation branch.

rows.stats: Same fighter name with no special mutation label
rows.use: Feed matching duplicates into the fighter selected for your active team.
Prioritize higher DPS when two fighters have similar rarity and attack speed.
Use longer-range fighters when enemies escape before short-range units can attack.
A lower cooldown means faster attacks and can outperform a slower fighter with similar damage.

rows.stats: Game pass advertised as a 200% higher chance of rolling mutated fighters
rows.use: Activate mutation-focused bonuses before spending a large amount of rolling currency.

merge-and-leveling-guide.title: Roll Anime to Fight Merge and Leveling Guide
merge-and-leveling-guide.intro: The merge system turns repeated rolls into permanent team power. Select your best version of a fighter first, preserve valuable mutations, and direct matching duplicates into the copy that will remain in your active team.

steps.title: Choose the Main Copy
steps.description: Compare rarity, mutation, DPS, range, and cooldown before selecting the fighter that will receive future merges.
steps.priority: Choose the strongest mutated or highest-performing copy

steps.title: Separate Mutation Branches
steps.description: Keep normal, Gold, Diamond, Nen, and Titan versions organized as separate fighter variants.
steps.priority: Do not consume a valuable mutation while upgrading a weaker normal copy

steps.title: Collect Matching Duplicates
steps.description: Continue rolling and save additional copies of fighters already used by your main team.
steps.priority: Team duplicates are more useful than unused low-impact fighters

steps.title: Merge to Raise the Level
steps.description: Use the merge interface to combine matching fighters and increase the selected fighter's displayed level and combat strength.
steps.priority: Concentrate merges on fighters that stay equipped during wave runs

steps.title: Check the Updated Stats
steps.description: After merging, compare the fighter's new DPS, range, and cooldown with the rest of the roster.
steps.priority: Keep the fighter equipped when the upgraded performance exceeds available replacements

steps.title: Replace Only for a Clear Upgrade
steps.description: A newly rolled fighter should replace a leveled unit only when its complete combat profile provides more useful wave damage.
steps.priority: A merged Legendary or mutated fighter can remain stronger than an unmerged higher-rarity pull

mutations-guide.title: Roll Anime to Fight Mutations Guide
mutations-guide.intro: Mutations create special versions of rolled fighters and can greatly improve the value of a strong unit. Mutation value depends on both the mutation and the base fighter carrying it, so a special mutation on a strong Mythic or Legendary fighter should normally be protected, equipped, and upgraded before ordinary copies.

mutations.bestFor: Core damage teams and high-wave progression
mutations.keepRule: Lock the fighter, equip it when its combat stats fit the team, and save matching Nen duplicates for merging.
Preserve Titan fighters as a dedicated upgrade branch and avoid using them as normal merge material.
Keep Diamond copies of useful fighters and merge them only with the matching Diamond branch.
Develop Gold fighters when they improve the active roster or provide a better merge path than the normal version.

mutations.bestFor: Starter teams and regular merge material
mutations.keepRule: Keep strong high-rarity fighters, but use extra normal copies to level the selected main copy.

waves-checkpoints-and-rewards.title: Roll Anime to Fight Waves Checkpoints and Rewards
waves-checkpoints-and-rewards.intro: Wave progression is the main test of team strength in Roll Anime to Fight. Improve the weakest part of the roster after each failed run, reach the next checkpoint, claim its reward, and reinvest those resources into rolling and merging.

stages.objective: Fill the active team and establish enough total damage to clear enemies consistently.
stages.teamCheck: Every available team position should contain a useful fighter.
stages.upgrade: Replace starter fighters with stronger rolled units.
stages.reward: Spend early earnings on additional rolls and immediate team improvements.

stages.objective: Clear multiple waves without enemies overwhelming the team.
stages.teamCheck: Damage should be spread across several consistent fighters instead of one carry surrounded by weak units.
stages.upgrade: Merge duplicates belonging to the active team.
stages.reward: Build one leveled fighter at a time.

stages.objective: Survive until the next checkpoint milestone and secure its progression reward.
stages.teamCheck: Compare total DPS, attack range, and cooldown after every failed attempt.
stages.upgrade: Upgrade the fighter contributing the least useful wave damage.
stages.reward: Reinvest checkpoint rewards into rolls that can produce upgrades or merge duplicates.

stages.objective: Maintain enough damage as enemy durability and pressure increase.
stages.teamCheck: The roster should contain leveled Legendary, Mythic, or valuable mutated fighters.
stages.upgrade: Develop Diamond, Nen, Titan, and other strong mutation branches.
stages.reward: Save large reward payouts for focused rolling and merging sessions.

stages.objective: Pass the previous highest wave and unlock a higher checkpoint.
stages.teamCheck: Avoid carrying unused low-level fighters that contribute little damage.
stages.upgrade: Replace the weakest slot before investing further in the strongest slot.
stages.reward: Use the milestone payout to strengthen the full team rather than one already dominant fighter.

stages.objective: Use the strongest available team to push from the highest checkpoint toward the final wave.
stages.teamCheck: Core fighters should be high rarity, merged to useful levels, and supported by valuable mutations.
stages.upgrade: Improve total team DPS while keeping practical range and fast cooldowns.
stages.reward: Continue the roll, merge, checkpoint, and reward loop until the final wave is cleared.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Roll Anime to Fight

questions.question: What is Roll Anime to Fight?
questions.answer: Roll Anime to Fight is a Roblox anime defense game where you roll anime fighters, build a team, merge duplicates to level them up, and survive endless enemy waves while chasing higher checkpoints and mutations.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Roll Anime to Fight?
questions.answer: Open the Codes option from the in-game menu, enter the code exactly as published, and press redeem. New Roll Anime to Fight codes are announced on the official Roblox experience page and the Another Slop community.

questions.question: How does merging work in Roll Anime to Fight?
questions.answer: Merging combines duplicate fighters to raise a fighter's level and combat strength. In Roll Anime to Fight you should concentrate merges on your best copy, separate valuable mutations into their own branches, and feed matching duplicates into the fighter that stays equipped.

questions.question: What are mutations in Roll Anime to Fight?
questions.answer: Mutations are special versions of rolled fighters such as Gold, Diamond, Nen, and Titan. In Roll Anime to Fight a strong mutation on a Mythic or Legendary fighter is usually worth protecting, equipping, and upgrading before ordinary copies.

questions.question: Where can I play Roll Anime to Fight?
questions.answer: Roll Anime to Fight is a free Roblox experience. You can launch it directly from the official Roblox game page on any device that supports Roblox.

questions.question: What is the best way to progress in Roll Anime to Fight?
questions.answer: Build a balanced team early, merge duplicate fighters into your strongest copies, push checkpoints for larger rewards, and reinvest those rewards into rolling and merging better units for higher waves.

cta.title: Ready to Play Roll Anime to Fight?
cta.description: Join the Another Slop community for Roll Anime to Fight updates and announcements, or jump straight into the game on Roblox.
